defect.summary: Both bounded runners can reap the direct child and then send a signal to the numerical process-group ID that was derived from the former child PID.
unsafe_sequence: The runner starts the direct child as a new process-group leader.
The runner calls poll or wait and can reap that leader.
The kernel can reuse the released numerical PID as an unrelated process-group ID.
The runner later calls killpg with the released number.
defect.setsid_case: A descendant can start a new session and retain an inherited output pipe. The direct leader can then exit. The original process group can cease to exist while the runner still waits for pipe closure. A later cleanup signal can use a recycled group ID.
defect.normal_completion_case: The success path waits for and reaps the leader before it calls killpg. This creates a process-group identity reuse window even when no descendant retains a pipe.
defect.violated_contract: A bounded verifier must not signal a process that does not belong to its invocation.
defect.consequence: CH-T003 epoch 1 cannot provide trustworthy bounded-process evidence or a trustworthy registered-verifier result.

decisive_observations.observation: The command function calls poll while it drains pipes, calls wait after the drain, and then calls killpg with process.pid.
The run_bounded function has the same poll, wait, and later killpg order.
The epoch 1 verifier and implementation plan are immutable F-stage values. An I-stage amendment cannot replace the registered verifier.
The signed protocol supports an INFLIGHT_ABORT at stage I. It restores every product path to the last verified snapshot and deactivates the defective registration.

steps: Append this verifier-defect record and deactivate CH-T003:e0001 without a history rewrite.
Restore all nine epoch 1 implementation paths from the CH-T002 activation snapshot.
Create CH-T003 epoch 2 with a registered runner that keeps the direct child identity reserved until process-group cleanup is complete.
Use the same identity-preserving order in the product runner.
Add tests that reject poll-before-cleanup, wait-before-cleanup, post-reap group signaling, retained same-group descendants, and unrelated-process signaling.

State that process-group cleanup cannot stop a descendant that starts a new session. Use the file and network sandbox to limit that residual case. Do not qualify host-resource containment or availability.
Run all signed F, I, C, and D checks for epoch 2 before any CH-T003 activation.
